disclaimer: SIMULATION OUTPUTS ARE SPECULATIVE. NOT AN INTELLIGENCE PRODUCT.

turns.label: Fragile Ceasefire (Apr 8-9)
real_events: Ceasefire takes effect between US and Iran
Iran begins tolling ships through Hormuz at $1M+ per vessel via IRGC coordination
China brokers commercial lane with yuan-denominated payments via Kunlun Bank
Russia and China veto UN Security Council resolution on Hormuz (11-2 vote)
3 Chinese-flagged commercial vessels transit via Chinese-brokered lane
Oil prices remain above $95/barrel

iran.description: Imposed toll regime on Hormuz transit, IRGC coordination required

usa.description: Maintained naval presence, mine-clearing operations continued

saudi.description: Exercised restraint, relied on Red Sea bypass pipeline

houthis.description: Held position, prepared Red Sea capabilities

shipping.description: Most ships remained anchored, few paid tolls to transit

turns.label: Talks & Posturing (Apr 10-12)
real_events: US Navy destroyers USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy transit Strait of Hormuz
IRGC issues 'last warning' to US warships in the strait
Islamabad peace talks convene with VP Vance leading US delegation
20-hour marathon negotiations at Islamabad
Talks collapse - US and Iran far apart on Hormuz control and nuclear program
Trump announces naval blockade of Iranian ports on Truth Social

iran.description: IRGC warned US warships, reinforced Hormuz defenses

usa.description: Destroyers transited Hormuz, Vance negotiated, then Trump declared blockade

israel.description: Continued striking Hezbollah in Lebanon during talks

china.description: Coordinated with Iran on commercial lane logistics

pakistan.description: Hosted talks, PM visited Saudi/Qatar/Turkey for support

shipping.description: Requested naval escort protection for commercial vessels

turns.label: Blockade (Apr 13-14)
real_events: US naval blockade begins at 10am ET on April 13
Oil prices jump 7% to $102/barrel
China calls blockade 'dangerous and irresponsible'
Iran vows retaliation, threatens to block all Gulf exports
Only 8 vessels transited strait on first day of blockade
US-sanctioned tanker linked to China attempts transit

iran.description: Threatened to completely block all Gulf exports and imports

usa.description: Implemented full naval blockade of all Iranian ports

israel.description: Maintained pressure on Hezbollah in Lebanon

saudi.description: Continued Red Sea bypass operations, braced for market impact

china.description: Condemned blockade diplomatically, Lavrov visited Beijing

pakistan.description: Continued backdoor diplomacy for second round of talks

houthis.description: Threatened to target Red Sea shipping and US warships

shipping.description: Suspended all Hormuz transits, traffic dropped to near zero

turns.label: Pressure Mounts (Apr 15-17)
real_events: CENTCOM declares blockade 'fully implemented' - Iran seaborne trade halted
Russia offers China energy lifeline through Lavrov-Xi meeting
Iran's oil storage filling up, wells may need to shut down
Iran threatens to block all Gulf/Red Sea/Oman traffic
Lebanon ceasefire declared (10-day truce between Israel and Hezbollah)
Iran announces Strait of Hormuz open for commercial vessels (April 17 evening)

iran.description: Opened Hormuz to commercial vessels under IRGC coordination

usa.description: Maintained blockade even after Iran announced opening

china.description: Accepted Russian energy offer, continued brokering Hormuz transit

pakistan.description: Field Marshal visited Iran, PM visited allies for second round

shipping.description: Cautiously welcomed opening but demanded safety guarantees

turns.label: Breaking Point (Apr 18-19)
real_events: Iran reverses course: closes Strait of Hormuz again citing US 'breach of trust'
IRGC gunboats fire on two merchant tankers (British tanker and container ship)
Ships that entered during brief opening forced to turn back
India summons Iran ambassador after Indian-flagged vessel threatened
Ceasefire deadline looms (expires April 21)
Trump says 'maybe I won't extend' ceasefire, threatens to 'start dropping bombs'
Iran parliament speaker says both sides 'far from final agreement'
Top US officials (Hegseth, CIA director) meet at White House

iran.description: Re-closed Hormuz, IRGC fired on tankers, reasserted full control

usa.description: Maintained blockade, Trump threatened to resume bombing

israel.description: Observed Lebanon ceasefire (with violations), focused on Iran terms

saudi.description: Braced for ceasefire expiry, continued bypass operations

china.description: Continued commercial engagement, offered to mediate

pakistan.description: Pushed for framework agreement before deadline

houthis.description: Prepared Red Sea attack capabilities for post-ceasefire

shipping.description: Full halt of transit attempts after ships fired upon
